title: Hedyotis trinervia
taxonomy: Hedyotis trinervia is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant is a member of the genus Hedyotis, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Hedyotis trinervia is a species of flowering plant that is native to India and Southeast Asia. It is a herbaceous perennial with small white flowers and is often found growing in woodland and grassland habitats. The classification of Hedyotis trinervia reflects its close relationship to other plants in the Rubiaceae family, which are characterized by their distinctive flowers and fruits.

intro: Hedyotis trinervia, also known as the Chinese chrysanthemum or three-nerved Hedyotis, is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Hedyotis trinervia include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and boost the immune system.
